page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9ae.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T95 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing a total of 16 vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for data persistence. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio offer a predictable resource profile for memory-bound workloads, avoiding the performance variability associated with shared-core or multi-threaded architectures.

Given its memory-optimized configuration of 128.0 GB RAM and dedicated AMD EPYC processor cores, the ecs.r9ae.4xlarge is designed for memory-intensive applications. Suitable workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and data processing pipelines that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9ae.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, delivering 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Designed without hyperthreading, the dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that require predictable CPU execution. It is suitable for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring significant memory capacity.
